engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Pajero Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Pajero across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Early Pajero workhorse; simple indirect injection, robust but prone to head gasket issues at high mileage.
compatibleVariants: 2.5 TD, 2.5 TD Fieldmaster, 2.5 TD GL, 2.5 TD GLX
commonFailures: Head gasket failure due to overheating (80k+ miles)
Fuel injector pump wear causing hard cold starts
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D56 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

entries.title: 2.8L Diesel
entries.history: The definitive Pajero diesel; replaced 4D56 with direct injection but introduced notorious timing chain issues.
compatibleVariants: 2.8 TD, 2.8 TD GLS, 2.8 TD GLX, 2.8 TD LWB, 2.8 TD SWB, 2.8 TD V63, 2.8 TD V73, Fieldmaster
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure causing "chuff chuff" noise at 2,500rpm (60k-100k miles)
Cylinder head cracking from overheating or pre-ignition
Injector pump seal leaks leading to cold start smoke
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Diesel
entries.history: Common rail evolution of 4M40; more power and refinement but complex fuel system demands specialist attention.
compatibleVariants: 3.2 DI-D, 3.2 DI-D GLS, 3.2 DI-D GLX, 3.2 DI-D LWB, 3.2 DI-D SGX, 3.2 DI-D SWB, 3.2 DI-D V73, 3.2 DI-D V78, Final Edition
commonFailures: Common rail injector failure causing misfires and loss of power (80k+ miles)
DPF clogging on post-2007 models with short-journey use
EGR cooler cracking leading to white smoke and coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4M41 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable SOHC V6 offering smooth power; interference design means timing belt failure causes catastrophic damage.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 V6, 3.0 V6 GLS, 3.0 V6 GLX, 3.0 V6 LWB, 3.0 V6 SWB
commonFailures: Timing belt failure causing valve damage if not replaced every 60k miles
Valve stem seal wear leading to blue smoke on startup (100k+ miles)
Oil consumption from worn piston rings on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G72 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

entries.title: 3.5L Petrol
entries.history: Larger DOHC V6 for performance variants; Evolution model featured twin turbos for rally-inspired power.
compatibleVariants: 3.5 V6, 3.5 V6 GLS, 3.5 V6 GLX, 3.5 V6 LWB, 3.5 V6 SGX, 3.5 V6 SWB, 3.5 V6 Evolution
commonFailures: Oil consumption from valve guide wear on high-mileage examples
Timing belt tensioner failure risking interference damage
Twin-turbo Evolution models: turbo seal failure and intercooler leaks
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G74 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Pajero Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Pajero's diesel engines are robust but not immune to age-related failures. The 4M40 timing chain, 4M41 injectors, and 4D56 head gaskets dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Shogun.

problems.h4: 4M40 Timing Chain Failure - Why "Chuff Chuff" Noise Means Urgent Action
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling at 2,500rpm and eventual jump/skip.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, guides, tensioner before catastrophic jump
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; does not fix pre-existing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip top end, check for valve/piston contact after chain jump
repairOptions.longevity: Only if damage is superficial; risk of hidden cracks remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M40 with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when chain has jumped; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: If you hear the characteristic "chuff chuff" at 2,500rpm, act immediately. On a Pajero worth under £4,000, a preventative chain kit from a specialist offers the best balance of cost and reliability. If the chain has already jumped, replacement with a rebuilt unit is almost always more economical than extensive repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4M40 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Pajero

problems.h4: 4M41 Injector Failure - Common Rail Complexity Drives Replacement Decisions
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail injectors wear internally, causing misfires, loss of power, and excessive fuel trim corrections.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if single injector failed; others may follow soon

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, pressure test rail, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Costly; often exceeds value of older Pajeros

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M41 with upgraded injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution for high-mileage or multiple injector failures

problems.recommendation: For post-2007 200PS 4M41 engines, injector failure is often a symptom of broader fuel system wear. If two or more injectors have failed, or the vehicle has over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components typically offers better value and reliability than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4M41 Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

problems.h4: 4D56 Head Gasket Failure - Classic Overheating Issue on Early Pajeros
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping from overheating or age, allowing coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip top end, replace gasket, skim head if flat
repairOptions.longevity: Only if head is undamaged; risk of recurrence if cause not addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, pressure test, machine, fit new valves/guides
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity but labour-intensive; cooling system must be addressed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4D56 with upgraded head, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for classic Pajeros kept long-term

problems.recommendation: On a 1980s Pajero, if the head is cracked or severely warped, replacement with a rebuilt unit is often more cost-effective than extensive machining. For vehicles with sentimental value or low mileage, a specialist head recondition may be worthwhile if the block is sound.
problems.cta: Compare reconditioned 4D56 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Pajero

problems.group: 4M40 Cold Start & Injector Pump Seal Leaks
problems.h4: 4M40 Cold Start & Injector Pump Seal Leaks - Fuel System Wear
problems.rootCause: Rubber seals on the rotary injection pump harden with age, allowing diesel to leak internally and causing hard cold starts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, replace internal seals, recalibrate timing
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if pump internals are sound; does not fix worn plungers

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip pump, replace worn components, test on bench
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity; recommended if pump has high mileage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M40 with reconditioned pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if pump failure coincides with other engine wear

problems.recommendation: Cold start issues with white smoke are classic 4M40 seal failure. If the engine otherwise runs well, a specialist pump recondition is cost-effective. However, if the vehicle has other age-related issues, a rebuilt engine may offer better long-term value.
problems.cta: Find Mitsubishi Pajero 4M40 fuel system specialists

problems.h4: 4M41 DPF & EGR Issues - Modern Diesel Emissions Complexity
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; EGR cooler cracks from thermal cycling, causing coolant loss and white smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, chemical clean or replace, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Only if DPF is clogged but not damaged; does not fix EGR issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked EGR cooler, flush cooling system, test for leaks
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses coolant loss but DPF may still fail if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M41 with emissions components addressed, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vehicles where multiple systems are worn

problems.recommendation: If your Pajero is used primarily for short trips, DPF/EGR issues are likely to recur. For vehicles over 100k miles, investing in a rebuilt engine with upgraded components often proves more economical than repeated emissions system repairs.

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Pajero
finalCta.paragraph: The Pajero's rugged reputation is well-earned, but age and mileage take their toll on even the toughest engines. Whether you're facing timing chain noise on a 4M40, injector failure on a 4M41, or head gasket issues on a classic 4D56, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Pajero specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get your Shogun back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Pajero Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Pajero and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Pajero Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Pajero is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Pajero 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L diesel powered the earliest UK Pajeros (1984-1991), offering simple, rugged performance for off-road enthusiasts. Its indirect injection design is easy to maintain but prone to head gasket issues at high mileage.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure from overheating; turbo oil seal leaks at 70k+ miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Pajero 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Pajero 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L 4M40 became the definitive Pajero diesel (1994-2006), balancing power and off-road capability. Its direct injection improved efficiency but introduced the notorious timing chain tensioner weakness.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing "chuff chuff" noise at 2,500rpm; injector pump seal leaks
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Pajero 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Pajero 3.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.2L 4M41 common rail diesel (2000-2014) delivered refined power for the final-generation Shogun. Its complexity brings higher replacement costs but excellent towing capability for serious off-roaders.
commonFailurePoints: Common rail injector failure; DPF clogging on post-2007 models; EGR cooler cracking
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Pajero 3.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Pajero 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L V6 offered smooth, accessible power for Pajeros prioritising on-road comfort. Its interference design demands strict timing belt maintenance to avoid catastrophic valve damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure causing valve damage; valve stem seal wear leading to blue smoke
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Pajero 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Pajero 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.5L V6 powered performance-oriented Pajeros, with the Evolution variant featuring twin turbos for rally-inspired output. Higher complexity means specialist knowledge is essential for replacement.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from valve guide wear; twin-turbo seal failure on Evolution models
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Pajero 3.5L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Pajero Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Pajero's UK production run, helping owners quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The first-generation Pajero established Mitsubishi's 4x4 reputation with simple, rugged diesel power.
keyChanges: Launch of UK-market Pajero with 2.5L 4D56 turbo diesel
Final year for 4D56 before Gen 2 redesign
knownFor: Head gasket failure common at high mileage
Classic off-road capability with basic electronics
years.cta: Get quotes for 1984-1991 Mitsubishi Pajero replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Pajero 2.5 TD engine replacement • 4D56 head gasket failure • Pajero Fieldmaster engine cost

years.description: The second generation introduced the legendary 4M40 2.8L diesel, defining the Pajero for a decade.
keyChanges: Replacement of 4D56 with new 4M40 2.8L direct injection diesel
Introduction of V63 chassis code for 2.8L models
Launch of GLS and GLX trim levels
knownFor: Strong off-road capability with upgraded suspension
years.cta: Get quotes for 1994-1999 Mitsubishi Pajero replacement engines
years.ticker: 4M40 timing chain rattle • Pajero 2.8 TD engine replacement • chuff chuff noise fix

years.description: The third generation brought common rail diesel technology, raising performance but adding complexity.
keyChanges: V73 chassis code for new-generation models
Launch of LWB and SWB body options
knownFor: Early common rail injector reliability concerns
Improved on-road refinement for family use
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2006 Mitsubishi Pajero replacement engines
years.ticker: 4M41 injector failure • Pajero 3.2 DI-D engine cost • common rail diesel repair

years.preview: Gen 3 LCI V78: High-Power 4M41 & Final Edition
years.description: The late-life facelift boosted the 4M41 to 200PS and added Euro 5 emissions compliance for the Final Edition.
keyChanges: 4M41 power increased to 200PS with revised turbo and ECU
Introduction of DPF and Euro 5 compliance (2007+)
Launch of Final Edition (2014) marking end of UK sales
knownFor: DPF and EGR issues on short-journey vehicles
Strong residual values due to limited supply
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2014 Mitsubishi Pajero replacement engines
years.ticker: 4M41 DPF clogged • Pajero Final Edition engine • 200PS diesel replacement cost

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi Pajero was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi Pajero specialists.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Pajero Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Pajero engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Pajero engines typically cost £2,400-£4,500 supply-only, depending on engine code. The 4D56 2.5L is most affordable, while 4M41 3.2L common rail units command premium pricing due to complexity.
keyPoints: 4D56 2.5L rebuilt: £2,400-£2,800 supply only
4M40 2.8L rebuilt: £2,600-£3,000 supply only
4M41 3.2L rebuilt: £4,300-£4,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £800-£1,500 typically
All prices include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement prices now

items.question: What causes the "chuff chuff" noise in my Mitsubishi Pajero 2.8 TD?
items.answer: The characteristic "chuff chuff" at 2,500rpm in 4M40 engines signals timing chain tensioner wear. Ignoring it risks chain jump, valve damage and catastrophic engine failure requiring full replacement.
keyPoints: Noise appears at steady 2,500rpm under light load
Caused by hydraulic tensioner losing pressure
Early intervention can prevent catastrophic damage
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioner components
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4M40 Mitsubishi Pajero engines

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Mitsubishi Pajero?
items.answer: For Pajeros in good structural condition with service history, a rebuilt engine often extends life cost-effectively versus buying another used 4x4. Consider vehicle value, mileage and your intended use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost 40-60% less than dealer replacements
Pajero chassis and drivetrain often outlive original engines
Specialist rebuilds address known failure points proactively
Warranty coverage reduces long-term ownership risk
items.cta: Find out if your Mitsubishi Pajero is worth an engine replacement

items.question: Which Mitsubishi Pajero engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 4M40 2.8L diesel offers the best balance of simplicity, parts availability and proven reliability when maintained properly. Avoid early 4M41 units with known injector concerns.
keyPoints: 4M40 benefits from simple mechanical injection design
Regular oil changes critical for timing chain longevity
4D56 is robust but prone to head gasket issues
4M41 requires specialist knowledge for repairs
items.cta: Compare reliability-focused rebuilt engines for your Pajero

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Pajero engine myself?
items.answer: While mechanically possible, Pajero engine replacement requires specialist tools, timing expertise and ECU coding knowledge. Most owners benefit from professional supply & fit services.
keyPoints: 4x4 chassis access complicates removal/installation
Timing procedures critical for 4M40/4M41 engines
ECU coding required for common rail 4M41 units
Specialist fitting ensures warranty validity
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Pajero engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Mitsubishi Pajero engine?
items.answer: Professional specialists typically complete Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement in 2-4 days, including removal, installation, timing, ECU coding and road testing.
keyPoints: 4M40/4M41 timing procedures add complexity
ECU coding required for post-2000 common rail models
Additional time needed if ancillaries require replacement
Nationwide delivery of rebuilt engines usually 24-48 hours
items.cta: Get a timeline estimate for your Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacement

items.question: Does a replacement Mitsubishi Pajero engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes. All rebuilt and reconditioned Mitsubishi Pajero engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms protect high-use off-road vehicles
Some suppliers offer extended warranty options
Always confirm exact terms before committing
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Mitsubishi Pajero engine replacements

items.question: Will a 4M40 engine fit my Mitsubishi Pajero V73 chassis?
items.answer: No. The 4M40 2.8L was fitted to V63 chassis (1994-2006), while V73 models (2000-2014) use the 4M41 3.2L. Engine codes must match chassis for ECU, wiring and mounting compatibility.
keyPoints: V63 chassis designed for 4M40 2.8L engine
V73 chassis requires 4M41 3.2L common rail engine
ECU, wiring loom and mounts differ between generations
Always verify engine code against your VIN before purchasing
items.cta: Check engine compatibility for your Mitsubishi Pajero chassis

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned and rebuilt Mitsubishi Pajero engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines receive cleaning, inspection and replacement of worn wear items. Rebuilt engines undergo full strip-down, machining and assembly to exceed factory specifications with upgraded components.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: cost-effective for engines with isolated failures
Rebuilt: comprehensive solution for high-mileage or complex failures
Both include 12-month warranty through EngineMarket network
Rebuilt units often include upgraded timing components for 4M40
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs rebuilt options for your Pajero
